title: codonopsis cardiophylla subsp. megaphylla
intro: Codonopsis cardiophylla subsp. megaphylla, also known as the giant codonopsis or giant tang-kuei, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Codonopsis, native to the mountainous regions of Sichuan and Yunnan provinces in China. This plant is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Codonopsis cardiophylla subsp. megaphylla include its ability to enhance immune function, reduce fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include dried roots, which can be made into teas, decoctions, or infusions, and are often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
